Tennessee Behavioral Health Safety Net Services

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Tennessee Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services

9,217 followers

1mo
  • Report this post

Uninsured and need outpatient mental health care? Tennessee’s Behavioral Health Safety Net provides essential services through community providers across all 95 counties. Services include: Assessment and evaluation Individual Family and group therapy Psychiatric medication management Labs related to medication management Pharmacy assistance and coordination Learn more at https://lnkd.in/eZCp9DGZ

    "We have a behavioral health waitlist, and my team is burning out trying to keep up." A clinic director said this to me recently while we were talking about their need for more Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioners (PMHNPs). I have these conversations a lot. Six weeks after adding the right PMHNP, that clinic's waitlist had been cut in half. Not because of a new policy. Not because of an awareness campaign. Because the right provider joined the team. That's what behavioral health access often looks like in real life. A clinic director finally getting relief after months of being short staffed. Patients being seen sooner. A care team finally able to breathe again. Awareness matters. But awareness doesn't see patients. As Mental Health Awareness Month closes, the most meaningful thing we can do is make sure the right providers are in the right places so the people who need care can actually get it. If your organization is navigating a similar challenge, we can help. #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #BehavioralHealth #PMHNP #MentalHealthCare #HealthcareRecruitment

Opening this summer, Meadowview Behavioral Hospital will serve the Peoria area and beyond with services for individuals seeking support for mental health and substance use related disorders.   • Michael Higginbotham
    1mo

    67 days. That's the median wait time for a psychiatric appointment in the United States right now. Only 18.5% of US psychiatrists are accepting new patients. If you've called a clinic, gotten the "we're booked through fall" voicemail, and wondered if you were doing it wrong — you weren't. The system is genuinely broken at the access layer. Just published a piece on the state of mental health access in Jackson County specifically. The harder data: - Oregon ranked 7th nationally for adult access (up from 21st five years ago — real progress) - Oregon ranked WORST nationally for prevalence of mental illness - Oregon ranked LAST nationally for youth mental health - Oregon's $221M Medicaid behavioral health rate increase in 2023 is the single biggest reason access has improved There's also a section on the four levers that actually move the needle — rate adequacy, workforce pipeline, rural telehealth, culturally responsive providers — and five concrete things you can do this week if you've been stuck trying to find care. Read the full breakdown → www.helpinghandtherapy.net #MentalHealthAccess #JacksonCountyOregon #SouthernOregon #BehavioralHealth #MentalHealthAdvocacy

  • Celina del Castillo

    The Fight for True Medical Accountability As we cross the midpoint of Mental Health Awareness Month, we must confront the stark division between standard "awareness" and the harsh reality of severe brain disorders. For decades, the public has been told that reducing stigma is the ultimate goal, but a family watching a loved one disappear into a cycle of untreated psychosis, homelessness, or incarceration knows that awareness does nothing to fix a broken system. The true crisis we face is a systemic Standard of Neglect. When our laws treat a biological medical emergency as a matter of personal choice, we are abandoning individuals whose brains are physically unable to comprehend that they are ill. Waiting for total deterioration or a tragic public headline before allowing clinical intervention is not compassion—it is institutional desertion. We are actively pushing our communities into the Era of Accountability. True progress requires a concrete National Standard of Care that treats the brain as a vital organ deserving of continuous, proactive medical attention. This means replacing fragmented crisis management with a functional clinical bridge that supports individuals all the way from hospital stabilization back into a thriving life. It is time to demand Medical Accountability and guarantee the Right to be Well for every individual. #MHAM2026 #MedicalAccountability #NationalStandardOfCare #StandardOfNeglect #SMI #NSSC #MedicalMercy #RightToBeWell #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #EraOfAccountability #SandyPruett

    Sacramento County DHS is seeking qualified providers to deliver the integrated ATLAS program, a comprehensive outpatient mental health service model for children and youth under 21. The program includes five coordinated components: specialty mental health services, therapeutic behavioral services, early intervention, outreach and engagement, and flexible stabilization supports. Providers must implement the full model under a single contract for five years. - Applications due by June 10, 2026, with a mandatory virtual applicants' conference on May 21. - The program emphasizes culturally competent, holistic care aligned with county values of respect, innovation, and inclusion. - Partial proposals will be rejected; full integration of all components is required. This initiative aims to enhance mental health access and outcomes for Sacramento’s youth. Read more: https://lnkd.in/g-dAUanv #PRESWERX #CaliforniaConstruction
